meaning: adj.教养浅薄的n.教养浅薄的人
examples: His choice of subject matter has been regarded as lowbrow.
Culture can be dealt with in two categories: "highbrow culture" and "lowbrow culture".
So far at least, commentaries in the official media have dwelt mainly on lowbrow entertainment on television and the Internet.
Kevin Reilly wants his network to get sophisticated--but won't let go of the lowbrow.
They used minimalist, separate folders for each class, usually Pee Chee folders modified to exhibit the best in lowbrow bathroom humor.
